expectations.text: Explains conditional certification: possible only if the assessment score is at least 88 (0.8 x 110) AND every NOT MET practice is POA&M-eligible
expectations.evidence: 'an organization can receive a Conditional Level 2 (C3PAO) Certification if: The organization's assessment score meets the minimum threshold (see below), and All NOT MET practices are practices that are POA&M-eligible (not on the excluded list), and...' with the score floor separately stated as 88/110.

expectations.text: States POA&M eligibility rules correctly: only practices worth 1 point may be on a POA&M (no 5-point items; 3-point items excluded except limited partial-credit cases), and names non-POA&M-eligible critical examples (e.g., SC.L2-3.13.11 FIPS crypto, AC.L2-3.1.3, IA.L2-3.5.3)
expectations.evidence: Response describes non-eligible practices only vaguely as '5-point and 3-point weighted controls' without stating that only 1-point items are POA&M-eligible, and its named examples are imprecise: it cites 'AC.L2-3.5.3 family' for MFA (the correct ID is IA.L2-3.5.3 — wrong domain prefix), lists SC.L2-3.13.11 (FIPS crypto, correct) and SC.L2-3.13.1 (boundary protection), but never names AC.L2-3.1.3 (CUI flow control) anywhere in the file. The assertion requires accurate naming of these specific critical examples; the MFA citation error and missing AC.L2-3.1.3 reference make this a miss on the specific requirement.

expectations.text: States the closeout: POA&M items must be remediated and verified by a closeout assessment within 180 days, or the conditional certification lapses
expectations.evidence: '180 calendar days is the standard maximum window to remediate all POA&M'd practices... If the 180 days lapse without closeout, the conditional certification expires and is not converted to a final certification.'

expectations.text: Explains the consequence math for THIS scenario: 8 NOT MET practices are compatible with conditional certification only if all 8 are 1-point items and the score floor holds — otherwise remediate and re-assess
expectations.evidence: 'If all 8 are 1-point practices: that's -8, landing you at 102/110 — likely still eligible. If several are 5-point practices (e.g., 3 x 5-point + 5 x 1-point = -20): that's 90/110 — borderline... If multiple 5-point "foundational" controls are involved, you risk both failing the score floor and hitting the non-eligible-for-POA&M list simultaneously.'

expectations.text: Mentions the annual senior-official affirmation in SPRS and that a lapsed conditional status affects contract eligibility
expectations.evidence: No mention of an annual senior-official affirmation, or of SPRS affirmation, anywhere in the response (verified via search — no matches for 'annual', 'affirm', or 'senior' in the file). The consequence of a lapse is described only as needing to 'restart the assessment process' and potentially 'jeopardize existing contract performance', without tying it to the affirmation mechanism.
